checks.name: confidence-threshold
checks.details: 3 rule(s) have confidence < 0.7: decelerator-outbound-below-quota, draw-recoverable, d2d-internal-director-comp

checks.name: duplicate-ids
checks.details: No duplicate rule IDs

checks.name: empty-parameters
checks.details: All rules have non-empty parameters

checks.name: concept-coverage
checks.details: Core concepts (rate-table, quota-target) are covered

checks.name: config-completeness
checks.details: CIQ config is complete

checks.name: rate-accuracy-digital-rate-table
checks.details: Digital.xlsx Digital Rate Table source data shows Sales ID 20,001,125 and 20,001,126 at $475 New Install Fiber / $380 New Install Copper, and 20,001,129 and 20,001,135 at $350 / $250. The extracted rule states Fiber new-install bounties range from $90 (Acquisition) to $500 (Updater Non-Brand). The $90 floor is unverifiable from the truncated source — minimum visible in source is $250. The $500 ceiling is confirmed (Sales IDs 20,001,122–124). Rule also claims 94 total Sales IDs; source header says 96 rows but row 1 is a header and Sales ID 20,001,140 appears missing between 20,001,139 and 20,001,141, suggesting a gap. Total Sales IDs may be 94 or 93 — requires full table verification.

checks.name: rate-accuracy-digital-vas-voice
checks.details: Digital Rate Table source confirms: Total Home Tech Support = $40, Wi-Fi Extender = $20, Wi-Fi Protection = $20, Inside Wire Protection = $0 for all visible Sales IDs. Voice products (Brightspeed 4G Voice, Residence Voice Line, Digital Voice-Standard) all confirmed at $50. These match the partner-vas-voice-rate-table Digital channel rates. Migration rate confirmed at $150 across all visible rows.

checks.name: rate-accuracy-mdu-ae-contract-type
checks.details: AE Model source confirms: base AE rate = $8.70, base Natl AE rate = $11.11. Multipliers: Access=0.7 (AE effective: 6.09 ✓), Shared=0.9 (AE effective: 7.83 ✓), Exclusive=1.1 (AE effective: 9.57 ✓), Bulk=1.4 (AE effective: 12.18 ✓). Natl AE derived rates (7.777, 9.999, 12.221, 15.554) confirmed from source row 'Rate Card (Contract Type)'. All effective rates match extracted rule within rounding.

checks.name: quota-consistency-mdu-ae
checks.details: AE Model source confirms: Per Rep = 383 units/month, Natl AE = 300 units/month, 8 AEs + 1 Natl AE = 3,364 units/month (8×383+300=3,364 ✓). Budget target = 3,726 (27 contracts × 138 units, confirmed by Sheet2 formula D3=27*138 ✓). Commission Targets row shows 3,353.4 = 90% × 3,726 (confirmed as Sr. Manager threshold, not direct target). Annual TSI $40K implied by '$40k per year commission' note in AE Calculator. Monthly TSI = $3,333.33 consistent with $40K/12. All quota values match extracted rules.

checks.name: quota-consistency-mdu-management-targets
checks.details: AE Model row '8 AEs + 1 Natl AE' = 3,364 confirms Sr. Manager AE target. Director AE target = 3,726 (Budget Target row). TSR team aggregate targets are seasonal per TSR Model. Source TSR Model formulas (F7=$E8*F8+$E9*F9) confirm weighted component structure. Sr. Manager target derivation '8×383 + 1×300 = 3,364' arithmetically confirmed.

checks.name: rate-accuracy-mdu-tsr-formula
checks.details: TSR Model formulas confirm two-component structure: F7=($E8*F8)+($E9*F9) where E8/E9 are weights and F8/F9 are component values. TSR Sales Feb Model formulas confirm: K17=I17/J17 (units sold attainment), O17=N17/M17 (current pen / prior pen). This matches extracted mdu-tsr-compensation-formulas rule exactly. 50/50 weight split is consistent with formula structure.

checks.name: rate-accuracy-spif-mdu-five-contract
checks.details: AE Model source confirms: '5 Contract Bonus (Exclusive or Bulk)' with threshold '5 Contracts that are Exclusive or Bulk with a min of 50 Contracted Units per contract' and bonus amount = $1,000 for both AE and Natl AE. Extracted rule matches exactly.

checks.name: rate-accuracy-cap-mdu
checks.details: AE Model source shows monthly TSI for AE = $3,333.33 (=$40K/12). A 300% cap would yield $10,000 cap amount — this matches the extracted rule. However, TSR monthly TSI is stated as $2,500/month in the rule, making 200% cap = $5,000 — also consistent. BUT the source AE Model row labeled 'Sr Mgr' shows monthly TSI of $5,000 (annualized $60K implied), and the Dir Model is referenced but not fully visible. Sr. Manager TSI of $5,000 × 200% cap = $10,000, but the rule does not specify a cap for Sr. Manager or Director roles. The cap-mdu-by-role rule only lists AE, Natl AE, TSR (MDU), TSR (SFU) — management caps are omitted.

checks.name: completeness-otb-advance-payment-structure
checks.details: OTB.xlsm JANUARY_OTB source shows columns 'Advance Payment (Amt previously Paid)' and 'Net Payment DUE' with values like Advance=$125, Net=-$125 and Advance=$650, Net=-$650 and Advance=$700, Net=$0. This advance/clawback mechanism is referenced in draw-recoverable and outbound-speed-rate-table rules but no dedicated rule captures the OTM advance payment calculation logic (Net = Advance - Previously Paid). The Partners_Summary sheet shows New Install total $147,375 with Cancel=-$43,140 for one partner. This clawback math is not fully codified in any extracted rule.

checks.name: completeness-otb-product-rate-values
checks.details: OTB.xlsm JANUARY_OTB source shows specific advance amounts: $125 (appears to be Migration/Voice rate), $650 (likely Fiber 1G New Install), $700 (likely Fiber 2G New Install), $35 (likely Wi-Fi Extender), $25 (likely Wi-Fi Protection). The conflict resolution note acknowledges OTB.xlsm values differ from speed-tiered plan. However, no extracted rule captures the OTM product-level rate mapping (product name → advance amount) used operationally. The outbound-speed-rate-table uses speed tiers, but OTB operates by product. This gap could cause CaptivateIQ implementation errors.

checks.name: completeness-mdu-ae-calculator-formula
checks.details: AE Calculator formulas show: E6=(B6*B3)*D6 where B3=C2/B2 (rate per unit derived from TSI/quota). This formula structure (Units × DerivedRate × ContractMultiplier) is not explicitly captured in any rule. The mdu-ae-contract-type-rate-table rule states 'Commission = Contracted Units × Rate per unit' which is correct, but the CaptivateIQ implementation needs the intermediate B3 calculation (TSI ÷ Quota = base per-unit rate). The formula E13=SUM(E6:E12) confirms per-contract summation.

checks.name: contradiction-digital-commission-event
checks.details: The commission-crediting-basis rule states Digital is 'currently Installed, noted as transitioning.' The digital-partner-rate-matrix rule states 'commissionEvent: Installed (transitioning from Issued).' However, DAP.xlsx has both an 'Issued' sheet (200 rows) and an 'Installed' sheet (200 rows), suggesting both are actively processed. The direction of transition is contradictory between rules: commission-crediting-basis says 'transitioning' (from Issued to Installed) while digital-partner-rate-matrix says 'transitioning from Issued to Installed' — these actually agree directionally but the current operative basis is ambiguous given both sheets are populated.

checks.name: formula-feasibility-mdu-tsr
checks.details: TSR Sales Feb Model formulas reference: I17/J17 (units sold / target), N17/M17 (current pen / prior pen from TSR Pen sample sheet). TSR Pen sample sheet is confirmed present with penetration decimal values (e.g., 0.2753). CIQ config includes 'Prior Month Penetration (percent)' as an Employee Assumption, confirming the prior_month_pen variable is planned. Formula feasibility confirmed.

checks.name: formula-feasibility-ae-feb-model-vlookup
checks.details: AE Feb Model formulas use VLOOKUP(D2,AgreeMap,2,FALSE) referencing named range 'AgreeMap' for contract type to multiplier mapping. CIQ config includes 'MDU Agreement Type Mapping (qualifier)' as an Attribute Worksheet, confirming this lookup table is planned. AE Jan Model similarly uses VLOOKUP(C2,AgreeMap,2,FALSE). Formula feasibility confirmed.

checks.name: formula-feasibility-dir-model-cross-sheet
checks.details: Dir Model formulas reference 'AE Model'!D9 through 'AE Model'!M9 (monthly targets row). CIQ config includes 'MDU Management Attainment (monthly)' component, but cross-sheet references require careful CaptivateIQ worksheet dependency ordering. The Director formula pulling from AE Model row 9 (Budget Target = 3,726) is consistent with the quota-mdu-management-targets rule Director aeTarget=3,726.

checks.name: contradiction-d2d-commission-timing-note
checks.details: The commission-crediting-basis conflict resolution states 'Issued basis is authoritative.' However, the Consolidation.xlsx note 'Currently Issued but moving to Install' was noted as potentially outdated. If this note is current documentation, it would mean D2D is scheduled to revert to Installed basis — directly contradicting the resolution. The extraction correctly flagged this but the resolution should be explicitly verified with the plan owner, as an incorrect basis would affect all D2D commission calculations.

checks.name: rate-accuracy-inside-wire-protection-zero
checks.details: Digital Rate Table source confirms Inside Wire Protection = $0 across all 19+ visible Sales IDs (20,001,122 through 20,001,141). Rule confirmed accurate. Clearlink OOA exception cannot be verified from available truncated data but is noted.

checks.name: completeness-dap-disconnected-sheet
checks.details: DAP.xlsx Disconnected sheet shows download speeds including 600,000 (600Mbps), 944,500 (~1Gbps), 2,000,000 (2Gbps), 1,000,000 (1Gbps), 15,000, 40,000 etc. The clawback-disconnect-rules rule applies to DAP channel but DAP.xlsx:Disconnected is listed as a source. No rule captures whether DAP disconnects link back to DAP Issued or DAP Installed records — the rule states tieback via InstallOrderKey/InstallOrderID but DAP commissions are earned on Installed basis, so the tieback field name may need to be 'InstalledOrderKey' for DAP specifically.

checks.name: confidence-threshold-low-confidence-rules
checks.details: 3 rules below 0.7 confidence threshold: (1) decelerator-outbound-below-quota (0.6) — penalty amount is TBD, making this rule non-implementable as-is. (2) draw-recoverable (0.6) — employee draw terms explicitly noted as unspecified. (3) d2d-internal-director-comp (0.65) — bonus is TBD and status is WIP. These rules should be flagged for deferral or marked as placeholder-only in CaptivateIQ until source data is obtained.

checks.name: rate-accuracy-otb-migration-rate
checks.details: OTB.xlsm JANUARY_OTB shows $125 as the advance amount for multiple rows under Sales ID 20,000,660. Partners_Summary shows 'Migration' column with value 48,655 and 'Cancel' of -43,140. The outbound-speed-rate-table extracted rule states migration rates are approximately 1/3.6–3.9x of new install rates. At the 0-300MB tier, new install=$450, migration=$125 — ratio is 3.6x ✓. However, $650 appears for a fiber product (likely 600MB–1G tier new install, plan rate=$650 ✓) and $700 appears (likely 1G–2G tier, plan rate=$700 ✓). The $125 migration rate appears flat across all speed tiers in OTB data, but the outbound-speed-rate-table rule implies tiered migration rates ($125, $150, $175, $200). This flat $125 in OTB is a potential mismatch requiring verification.

flaggedRules.reason: Confidence 0.6; penalty amount is explicitly 'TBD' with no numeric value. Rule cannot be implemented in CaptivateIQ without the actual reduction multiplier or floor amount. The attainment formula is present but the output of below-quota scenario is undefined.
flaggedRules.suggestion: Obtain the below-quota penalty rate from Requirements.xlsx Functional Requirements page P (referenced but not extracted). Until resolved, mark this component as a placeholder with $0 output and document the dependency. Do not deploy to production without this value.

flaggedRules.reason: Confidence 0.6; employee draw terms (amount, recovery period, recovery method) explicitly noted as 'Not specified in available documents.' Rule conflates two distinct mechanisms: OTM advance (well-defined) and employee draw (undefined). Mixing these in one rule risks incorrect implementation.
flaggedRules.suggestion: Split into two rules: (1) 'otm-advance-on-issuance' for the OTM advance/clawback model (well-supported by OTB.xlsm data), and (2) 'draw-employee-recoverable' as a stub pending source data from Requirements.xlsx Business Requirements A41 full context. Remove the combined rule from active CaptivateIQ config until employee draw terms are confirmed.

flaggedRules.reason: Confidence 0.65; bonus component is 'TBD' and rule is marked 'WIP.' Component weights (70/15/15) are extracted but no rate, target, or payout formula is defined for any of the three components. This is effectively a structural placeholder.
flaggedRules.suggestion: Defer implementation entirely. Create a documentation stub in CaptivateIQ with the weight structure but no active formula. Flag for plan owner sign-off before FY2026 D2D Director payroll processing.

flaggedRules.reason: OTB.xlsm operational data shows flat $125 migration rate across multiple speed tiers, contradicting the rule's implication of tiered migration rates ($125/$150/$175/$200). Source conflict resolution acknowledges OTB vs Consolidation discrepancy but does not resolve the migration rate tier question specifically.
flaggedRules.suggestion: Verify whether OTM migration commission is truly flat $125 regardless of speed tier (as OTB data suggests) or tiered (as Consolidation plan design implies). If flat, update outbound-speed-rate-table migration rates to {all tiers: 125}. If tiered, investigate why OTB shows uniform $125 — possibly OTB sample is all sub-300MB orders.

flaggedRules.reason: Rule states totalSalesIds=94 but source data shows a gap at Sales ID 20,001,140 (sequence jumps from 20,001,139 to 20,001,141). Digital Rate Table header indicates 96 rows; minus 1 header row = 95 data rows. If 20,001,140 is genuinely absent and there are no other gaps, actual count may be 94 or 95. The $90 minimum Fiber rate (Acquisition) cannot be verified from visible source data — minimum visible is $250.
flaggedRules.suggestion: Run a full COUNT(DISTINCT SalesID) on Digital.xlsx Digital Rate Table to confirm exact count. Verify the $90 Acquisition Fiber rate exists in the untruncated table. Update totalSalesIds to verified count. If $90 rate is not found, revise the Fiber new-install range floor to $250.

flaggedRules.reason: Rule covers AE, Natl AE, TSR (MDU), TSR (SFU) caps but omits Sr. Manager AE, Sr. Manager TSR, and Director caps. Source MDU.xlsx references Dir Model and Sr. Manager compensation structures but cap multipliers for management are not extracted.
flaggedRules.suggestion: Extract cap rules from MDU.xlsx Dir Model!A8:H9 and TSR Model management rows. Add cap entries for SrManagerAE, SrManagerTSR, and Director to the cap-mdu-by-role rule params. If no cap applies to management, explicitly document capType='No Cap' for those roles to prevent ambiguity.

flaggedRules.reason: Rule specifies tieback fields as InstallOrderKey/InstallOrderID, but DAP channel earns commission on Installed basis — the original record may be identified differently than D2D (which is Issued). Using InstallOrderKey generically across channels may cause incorrect tieback for DAP disconnects if DAP uses a different primary key.
flaggedRules.suggestion: Verify tieback field names per channel: D2D uses IssuedOrderKey (since commission basis is Issued); DAP/Digital uses InstalledOrderKey (commission basis is Installed); Indirect uses InstalledOrderKey. Update tiebackFields to be channel-specific in the rule params.

flaggedRules.reason: Consolidation.xlsx note 'Currently Issued but moving to Install' for D2D creates directional ambiguity. The conflict resolution assumes this is outdated, but if it is a forward-looking plan note dated after 9/7/2025, it would mean D2D is expected to revert to Installed basis in a future period — a major operational change that would invalidate the Issued basis assumption.
flaggedRules.suggestion: Escalate to plan owner for explicit confirmation of D2D commission basis as of current period (Week of 2/1/2026). Document the decision in the rule with an effective date range. Add a review checkpoint for Q2 2026 if the Consolidation note reflects a planned future transition.

validatedRules.description: D2D channel internet commission rates tiered by download speed and partner group (D2D vs Indirect-D2D). Rates are flat per-unit bounties paid on order issuance. Indirect-D2D partners receive a $25-$50 premium at 600Mbps–2Gbps tiers. Both groups converge at $675 above 5Gbps. Same rate applies to New Install and Migration orders.

parameters.lookupMethod: XLOOKUP approximate match on MaxSpeed filtered by PartnerGroup

validatedRules.description: Indirect Sales channel internet commission rates tiered by download speed, with distinct rates for New Install vs Migration orders. Commission is earned on installation (not issuance). Migration rates are 17-33% lower than New Install at every tier.

parameters.lookupMethod: XLOOKUP approximate match on MaxSpeed filtered by OrderType

validatedRules.description: Outbound (OTM) channel internet commission rates tiered by download speed. New Install rates are 3.6-3.9x Migration rates, heavily incentivizing new customer acquisition. Commission is earned on order issuance, processed monthly.

Digital channel (DAP/Dot.Digital) commission rate matrix: ~94 Sales IDs across 6 partners and 4 team tiers (Acquisition, Branded, Non-Branded, Non-Paid Media) with 10 product rate columns. Rates are flat per-unit bounties. Fiber new-install bounties range from $90 (Acquisition) to $500 (Updater Non-Brand). Revised 12/8/2025.

specialCases.note: Only Fiber new install at $125; all other products $0
All products 'invoiced' - paid via separate invoice, not commission table

sourceRef.vendorRuleId: Digital.xlsx:Digital Rate Table!A2:P96, Consolidation.xlsx:Plan Info!A2:I42, Consolidation.xlsx:Rate Tables!B47:Q141

validatedRules.description: Consolidated VAS and Voice commission rates across all partner channels. Voice products (Digital Voice, Residence Voice, Fiber Voice, 4G Voice) uniformly pay $50 in D2D/Indirect/Outbound and $20-50 in Digital depending on team tier. WiFi Extender pays $20-35, WiFi Protection $20-25, Tech Support $40-50. Inside Wire Protection universally pays $0.

parameters.lookupMethod: XLOOKUP on RateKey (Product | PartnerGroup or Product | OrderType)

sourceRef.vendorRuleId: D2D.xlsx:Lookup!C33:F46, Indirect.xlsx:Lookup!C23:F36, Digital.xlsx:Digital Rate Table, Consolidation.xlsx:Plan Info

validatedRules.description: MDU AE and National AE per-unit commission rates varying by contract/agreement type. A base per-unit rate is multiplied by a contract-type multiplier. AE base rate $8.70; Natl AE base rate $11.11. Commission = Contracted Units × Rate per unit.

sourceRef.vendorRuleId: MDU.xlsx:AE Model!B12:F18, AE Model!B40:E47, AE Feb Model!Q3:R8

validatedRules.description: MDU TSR compensation has two components: (1) Units Sold at 50% weight, calculated as attainment% × TSI allocation, and (2) Portfolio Penetration at 50% weight, calculated as (current month pen / prior month pen) × TSI allocation. Both MDU and SFU TSR roles use this structure.

parameters.notes: Penetration ratio > 1.0 earns above TSI; < 1.0 earns below TSI

sourceRef.vendorRuleId: MDU.xlsx:TSR Model!A12:G14, TSR Sales Feb Model!H17:L31

validatedRules.description: MDU management compensation structures. AE Sr. Manager earns 100% TSI on team unit attainment. TSR Sr. Manager earns 50% on team units sold + 50% on team penetration. Director earns 50% on AE units contracted + 25% on TSR penetration + 25% on TSR units sold.

sourceRef.vendorRuleId: MDU.xlsx:AE Model!B28, TSR Model!A14, Dir Model!A8:H9

validatedRules.description: MDU individual quota targets by role. AE has flat 383 units/month ($40K annual TSI). Natl AE has flat 300 units/month ($40K annual TSI). TSR MDU has seasonal targets (300–495/month, $2,500/month TSI). TSR SFU has seasonal targets (300–400/month, $2,500/month TSI). Budget baseline is 27 contracts × 138 units = 3,726 units/month.

MDU management-level aggregate targets. AE Sr. Manager: 3,364 units/month (sum of 8 AE × 383 + 1 Natl AE × 300). Director: 3,726 units/month AE budget + seasonal TSR team aggregate (1,200–1,885/month).

sourceRef.vendorRuleId: MDU.xlsx:AE Model!B8:P9, TSR Model!A7:Q7, Dir Model!C16

validatedRules.description: Commission earning event differs by channel. D2D: pay on Issued (changed from Installed effective 9/7/2025). Indirect: pay on Installed. Outbound/OTM: pay on Issued (advance on order, monthly clawback). Digital: currently Installed, noted as transitioning. All channels use OrderDate >= 9/6/2025 as cutoff for current program eligibility.

parameters.cutoffRule: Orders with OrderDate prior to 9/6/2025 are excluded from current program eligibility

validatedRules.description: Copper technology orders are excluded from D2D commission eligibility. Only Fiber technology products are commissionable in the D2D channel. Eligibility is set to FALSE with FailCode=1 when Technology='Copper'.

Product name to rate column mapping for the Digital channel. Determines which rate table column applies based on OrderType, Technology, and ProductName. 12 Fiber products map to 'New Install Fiber', 10 Copper products to 'New Install Copper', 4 Fiber products with MIGRATION OrderType to 'Migrations', and 7 VAS/Voice products to their respective columns.

parameters.voiceConsolidation: Three voice columns (4G Voice, Residence Voice, Digital Voice Standard) consolidated into single 'VOIP' type at same rate in normalized view

sourceRef.vendorRuleId: Digital.xlsx:Digital Product Name Mapping!A2:D34, Digital Rate Table - Normalized!A1:C752

validatedRules.description: Orders flagged by OWS (Order Workflow System) compliance review are marked 'OWS - Do Not Pay' and commission is waived to $0. Applied as a post-calculation adjustment reducing NetCommissionAmount. Affects D2D and Indirect channels.

Cancel clawback rules across all partner channels. Canceled orders trigger a negative chargeback equal to the original commission. D2D has a 90-day lookback window. Outbound and Digital Partners claw back the entire amount in following month(s). Certain cancel reasons waive the chargeback (company-fault cancellations).

Outbound.method: Entire amount clawed back in following month/months

Digital.method: Entire amount clawed back in following month/months

validatedRules.description: Disconnect clawback rules. When a previously installed order is disconnected, the full commission is clawed back as a negative charge. Unlike cancel chargebacks, disconnect chargebacks are NEVER waived (CBWaived = FALSE hardcoded) in D2D. The disconnect is linked to the original install order via InstallOrderKey/InstallOrderID for traceability.

parameters.calculation: Negative of original rate-table commission amount
parameters.waiverLogic: CBWaived = FALSE (always; hardcoded for D2D disconnects)

validatedRules.description: MDU monthly commission caps by role, expressed as a multiplier of the monthly TSI. AE and Natl AE are capped at 300% of TSI; all other roles capped at 200% of TSI. No minimum earning floor for any role.

sourceRef.vendorRuleId: MDU.xlsx:AE Model!B26:G28, TSR Model!A12:G14, Dir Model!A8:H9

validatedRules.description: All partner commission plans (Indirect, Outbound, Digital Partners) have no payout cap. D2D cap status is not explicitly stated but no cap is found in any operational workbook.
MDU AE or National AE earns a $1,000 bonus for executing 5 or more contracts in a month that are Exclusive or Bulk agreement types, with a minimum of 50 contracted units per contract.

Multi-tier credit hierarchies by channel. DAP/Digital uses 4 levels: Channel → SalesPartner → SubAgent → AgentID. SubAgent names encode tier structure (Tier 1/2/3/MISC) that maps to rate differentiation. OTM uses 3 levels: SalesPartner → SubAgent → AgentID. Indirect uses 2 levels: SalesPartner → SubAgent. Commission is paid at the SalesPartner level with SubAgent tracked for reporting.

Orders are geographically assigned to WireCenters (identified by CLLI codes) across Brightspeed's multi-state footprint spanning 17+ US states. WireCenters serve as the territory unit linking customer addresses to service areas and partner coverage. No explicit vendor-to-state restriction is visible; multiple vendors operate across the same geographies.

D2D sales partners are mapped to one of two commission rate groups (D2D or Indirect-D2D) via the tblPartnerGroup lookup table. The partner group determines which rate column of the speed rate table applies.

Unified rate selection logic across D2D and Indirect channels: if order has a Download Speed value, use the speed-based rate table (tbl_speedrate); if Download Speed is blank, use the product-based rate table (tbl_productrate). RateKey is derived as CONCAT(ProductKey, ' | ', PartnerGroup/OrderType).

parameters.logic: IF(DownloadSpeed is blank, XLOOKUP in tbl_productrate by RateKey, XLOOKUP in tbl_speedrate by MaxSpeed filtered to PartnerGroup/OrderType)

validatedRules.description: Commission processing periods by channel. D2D and Indirect process weekly (Sunday-Saturday). OTM/Outbound processes monthly with payment ~13 days into following month. MDU processes monthly. Current period observed: Week of 2/1/2026–2/7/2026 for D2D/Indirect; January 2026 for OTM (paid Feb 13).

D2D Partner plan eligibility conditions: opportunity must have install/issue date in payment period, partner must be Active (agent status irrelevant), products must have effective rate, and partner must be assigned to a compensation plan.

conditions: Opportunity must have an install/issue date in the payment period
Partner must be Active at time opportunity is closed
Products must be assigned a rate effective on the install date
Partner must be assigned to a compensation plan at time of credit eligibility
parameters.trueUp: Current quarter only; previous quarter true-up no longer exists
parameters.payoutFormula: Sum commission payout + payment adjustments + NRD payments + any previous period commission owed

validatedRules.description: Variable data change effective dating rule for Employee plans: changes on or before the 12th of the month are backdated to the 1st; changes on/after the 13th are effective from the 1st of the following month. If only non-structural variable changes, prorate by business day.

parameters.variableOnlyChange: Prorate by business day: (old_variable / biz_days_in_month × biz_days_on_old) + (new_variable / biz_days_in_month × biz_days_on_new)

validatedRules.description: Outbound Partner channel applies a penalty for performance below quota threshold. Exact reduction amount or multiplier is not specified in available documents.

parameters.attainmentFormula: ARR / Quota = Attainment; Attainment × Weighted Variable = Payout

validatedRules.description: Recoverable draw exists for certain Employee plans. OTM partners receive advance payments on order issuance that are recovered via monthly clawback on cancellation, functioning as a de facto advance/draw model. Detailed terms (amount, recovery period, recovery method) for employee draws are not specified in available documents.

SDR plans are the only plans with rollup crediting to manager. SDR Mentors and SDR Managers receive a flat rate (multiplier hardcoded at 1, no acceleration). SDR Manager earns the same rate as 'Opp Found By'.

SDR_Manager.rateSource: Same Rate as Opp Found By

validatedRules.description: Employee plan SPIFs and kickers: (1) EDU Kicker provides additional commission for EDU-flagged opportunities, mutually exclusive with standard kickers. (2) Pacing Kicker is not subject to true-ups. (3) Sales Engineer Enterprise and SMB plans have SPIFF components.

eduKicker.eligibility: Opp Owner ID where EDU Kicker = True on Global Attributes

validatedRules.description: Commission eligibility varies by OrderType across all channels. NEW INSTALL and MIGRATION are commissionable events. DISCONNECT triggers clawback. Cancellation reasons are categorized to determine whether chargebacks are applied or waived.
D2D Director internal employee compensation with three weighted components: 70% Gross Sales Target, 15% Cancel Rate, 15% Speed Mix. Bonus TBD. This is separate from the partner payout structure.
Base Commission Rate (BCR) formula for Employee plans: BCR = Target Variable Pay (USD) × GARR Weighting ÷ Annual GARR Quota. Uses constant currency exchange rate set at beginning of fiscal year.

parameters.formula: BCR = Target Variable Pay (USD) × GARR Weighting ÷ Annual GARR Quota
parameters.currencyHandling: Constant Currency exchange rate set at beginning of year from Snowflake data workbook

validatedRules.description: Inside Wire Protection product pays $0 commission across ALL channels and ALL partners (effectively a non-commissionable product). The only exception is Clearlink OOA which is invoiced separately. Product is included in tracking/reporting but universally zero-rated.

parameters.applicability: All channels, all partners except invoiced OOA

sourceRef.vendorRuleId: Digital.xlsx:Digital Rate Table!P3:P96, Consolidation.xlsx:Plan Info, D2D.xlsx/Indirect.xlsx not listed

planStructure.notes: This plan spans 5 distinct channels with different processing frequencies, crediting bases, and rate structures. Recommend implementing as 5 separate CaptivateIQ plans sharing common data sources and attribute worksheets. D2D and Indirect share similar structure (speed-tiered rates, clawback logic) but differ in crediting basis (issued vs installed) and rate levels. Digital Partners use a fundamentally different rate structure (matrix by Sales ID). MDU is an internal employee plan with quota-based attainment.

dataWorksheets.name: D2D Speed Rate Table
dataWorksheets.description: Speed-tiered internet commission rates for D2D channel with two partner group columns

apiPayload.name: D2D Speed Rate Table
apiPayload.description: Commission rate lookup by speed tier and partner group for D2D channel

dataWorksheets.name: Indirect Speed Rate Table
dataWorksheets.description: Speed-tiered internet commission rates for Indirect channel with New Install and Migration columns

apiPayload.name: Indirect Speed Rate Table
apiPayload.description: Commission rate lookup by speed tier and order type for Indirect channel

dataWorksheets.name: Outbound Speed Rate Table

formulaRecommendations.logicExplanation: For each order, determine if it has a download speed. If yes, look up the speed-tiered rate from the appropriate rate table filtered by partner group (D2D) or order type (Indirect). If no speed, look up the flat product rate from the VAS/Voice table. D2D uses partner group dimension; Indirect uses order type dimension.
formulaRecommendations.pseudoFormula: IF(DownloadSpeed IS BLANK, LOOKUP(Product + '|' + PartnerGroupOrOrderType, VAS_Voice_Rate_Table.Rate), LOOKUP_APPROX(DownloadSpeed, IF(Channel='D2D', D2D_Speed_Rate_Table[PartnerGroup_Column], Indirect_Speed_Rate_Table[OrderType_Column])))
formulaRecommendations.captivateiqNotes: Use SmartGrid VLOOKUP with approximate match for speed-tiered rates. Create two lookup columns in the Payout Worksheet — one for speed-based and one for product-based — then use IF to select. Filter the rate table by PartnerGroup or OrderType before lookup.

formulaRecommendations.logicExplanation: For Digital/DAP channel, lookup is by SalesID. First determine which rate column applies using the Product Name Mapping (OrderType + Technology + ProductName → RateColumn). Then look up the rate from the Digital Partner Rate Matrix at the intersection of SalesID and RateColumn.
formulaRecommendations.pseudoFormula: LET(rateColumn = LOOKUP(OrderType + Technology + ProductName, DigitalProductMapping.RateColumn), LOOKUP(SalesID, DigitalPartnerRateMatrix[rateColumn]))
formulaRecommendations.captivateiqNotes: Implement as a two-step lookup in SmartGrid. Step 1: Map the product to a rate column using the Product Name Mapping worksheet. Step 2: Retrieve the rate from the Digital Partner Rate Matrix for the given SalesID and rate column. Consider using the normalized (unpivoted) rate table format for simpler VLOOKUP.

formulaRecommendations.logicExplanation: Apply a series of eligibility gates: (1) Technology != 'Copper' for D2D, (2) OrderDate >= 2025-09-06 for all channels, (3) Channel matches plan assignment, (4) Partner is Active, (5) Compliance flag is not 'OWS - Do Not Pay'. Return Eligible=TRUE only if all conditions pass; otherwise set appropriate FailCode.
formulaRecommendations.pseudoFormula: Eligible = AND(IF(Channel='D2D', Technology != 'Copper', TRUE), OrderDate >= '2025-09-06', PartnerStatus = 'Active', ComplianceFlag != 'OWS - Do Not Pay')
formulaRecommendations.captivateiqNotes: Implement as a calculated boolean column in the Payout Worksheet. Chain multiple IF conditions. Use a FailCode integer column alongside for debugging/audit. Apply as a multiplier (0 or 1) to the commission amount.

formulaRecommendations.logicExplanation: For canceled orders: look up the rate that would have been earned, negate it, then check if the ConsolidatedReason is in the waiver list. If waived, commission = $0. For disconnects: always apply full negative chargeback (no waiver). D2D has a 90-day lookback window. Classify as 'Prior Period' or 'Same Period' based on OrderDate vs WeekStart.
formulaRecommendations.pseudoFormula: CommissionAmountPre = -1 * RateLookup(product, speed, orderType); CBWaived = IF(EventType='Cancel', ISIN(ConsolidatedReason, CancelWaiverReasons), FALSE); FinalAmount = IF(CBWaived, 0, CommissionAmountPre); PeriodType = IF(OrderDate < WeekStart, 'Prior Period', 'Same Period')
formulaRecommendations.captivateiqNotes: Create separate commission event rows for chargebacks (negative amounts). Use a CommissionEvent column to distinguish 'Chargeback Cancel' from 'Chargeback Disconnect'. Apply waiver logic via MATCH against the Cancel Waiver Reasons data worksheet. For D2D 90-day lookback, filter chargebacks where CancelDate - OrderDate <= 90.

formulaRecommendations.logicExplanation: For each AE/Natl AE deal: map Offer Type to Agreement Category, look up the per-unit rate for the role+agreement, multiply by contracted units. Sum across all deals in the month. Apply 300% TSI cap via MIN function. Add $1,000 bonus if 5+ Exclusive/Bulk contracts with 50+ units each.
formulaRecommendations.pseudoFormula: DealCommission = ContractedUnits * LOOKUP(Role + AgreementCategory, MDU_Rate_Table.EffectiveRate); MonthlyTotal = SUM(DealCommissions); BonusContracts = COUNTIFS(AgreementCategory IN ('Exclusive','Bulk'), ContractedUnits >= 50); Bonus = IF(BonusContracts >= 5, 1000, 0); FinalPayout = MIN(MonthlyTotal + Bonus, 3 * MonthlyTSI)
formulaRecommendations.captivateiqNotes: Use a SmartGrid formula to map each deal through the agreement type mapping, then look up the rate. Aggregate at the rep-month level. Apply the cap as MIN(calculated, 3 * TSI). The 5-contract bonus requires a COUNTIFS across the rep's monthly deals — implement as a separate calculation row or component.

formulaRecommendations.logicExplanation: Two components each at 50% TSI: (1) Units Sold: attainment% = units_sold / target, commission = TSI_Sales * attainment%. (2) Penetration: pen_ratio = current_month_pen / prior_month_pen, commission = TSI_Pen * pen_ratio. Total = component1 + component2. Apply 200% TSI cap.
formulaRecommendations.pseudoFormula: SalesComm = TSI_Sales * (UnitsSold / MonthlyTarget); PenComm = TSI_Pen * (CurrentMonthPen / PriorMonthPen); TotalComm = MIN(SalesComm + PenComm, 2 * MonthlyTSI)
formulaRecommendations.captivateiqNotes: Create two component columns in the Payout Worksheet. TSI_Sales and TSI_Pen are each 50% of Monthly TSI from Employee Assumptions. Prior Month Penetration should be an Employee Assumption column updated monthly. Apply cap at the total level after summing components.

formulaRecommendations.description: Outbound/OTM Commission with Advance and Clawback
formulaRecommendations.logicExplanation: Commission is advanced at order issuance based on the speed-tiered rate table. If the order is subsequently canceled, the full advance is clawed back as a negative amount in the following month's payout. Monthly payout = New Install commissions + Migration commissions - Cancel clawbacks + Dispute adjustments.
formulaRecommendations.pseudoFormula: AdvancePayment = LOOKUP_APPROX(DownloadSpeed, OutboundSpeedRateTable[OrderType_Column]); NetPaymentDue = IF(OrderStatus = 'Canceled', -1 * AdvancePayment, AdvancePayment); MonthlyPayout = SUM(NetPaymentDue) BY Vendor
formulaRecommendations.captivateiqNotes: Each order generates either a positive commission (new install/migration) or a negative clawback (cancel). Aggregate by vendor/month for the payout worksheet. Track the OriginalPaymentDate to link clawbacks to their original advance period.

formulaRecommendations.logicExplanation: After commission is calculated, check the Compliance table for orders flagged 'OWS - Do Not Pay'. If flagged, set CommissionWaived = TRUE and NetCommissionAmount = 0. Track the waived amount separately for reporting.
formulaRecommendations.pseudoFormula: ComplianceFlag = LOOKUP(OrderID, ComplianceTable.Flag); NetCommission = IF(ComplianceFlag = 'OWS - Do Not Pay', 0, CommissionAmount); WaivedAmount = CommissionAmount - NetCommission
formulaRecommendations.captivateiqNotes: Implement as a final adjustment column in the Payout Worksheet. Join the Compliance data source by OrderID. The waived amount should appear as a separate line item on the partner statement for transparency.

insights: The extraction is high quality overall, with 30 rules covering D2D, Indirect, Outbound, Digital, and MDU channels comprehensively. Core rate tables, quota structures, clawback logic, and qualifier rules are well-supported by source data. The Digital Rate Table rates (VAS/Voice, Inside Wire Protection = $0) and MDU AE contract-type multipliers are precisely confirmed. MDU TSR and AE formula structures are correctly derived from workbook formulas. The primary quality concerns are: (1) Three low-confidence rules (decelerator-outbound, draw-recoverable, d2d-director-comp) are non-implementable placeholders that should not enter production config; (2) OTM migration rate appears flat at $125 in operational data vs. tiered in plan design — this is a material discrepancy requiring business owner resolution before CaptivateIQ deployment; (3) Management-level MDU caps are missing from cap-mdu-by-role; (4) The D2D commission basis ambiguity (Issued vs. future revert to Installed) is a high-stakes unresolved item that would affect every D2D commission calculation if incorrectly set; (5) The disconnect clawback tieback field logic is channel-agnostic when it should be channel-specific given D2D uses Issued basis. The known formula bug in Indirect.xlsx Disconnected sheet (AI2 referencing tbl_canceled) is correctly flagged and should be the first fix validated in CaptivateIQ UAT. Recommend a targeted review of OTB.xlsm full migration rate data and MDU.xlsx Dir Model caps before proceeding to CaptivateIQ build.
